function.name: _bpmn_create_or_update_flow
function.description:
Create or update detailed configuration for a sequence flow in the workflow. Includes name, start/end nodes, condition expressions, and descriptions. [Minimum Process Topology Rules]
- Each process must have at least one sequence flow starting from the [StartEvent].
- If a StartEvent exists in the process but no outgoing SequenceFlow is configured, AI must prioritize creating a sequence flow from StartEvent to the first business node or gateway.
- Prohibit generating process structures with "start node but no connections".
- This rule is a process structure-level strong constraint with higher priority than business rules.

flow.description: Detailed information of the sequence flow to be created or updated.

id.description: Sequence flow ID, corresponding to BPMN sequenceFlow id, e.g.: Flow_Approve_1.

name.description: Sequence flow name, used for the name attribute in BPMN.

sourceRef.description: Start node ID, corresponding to BPMN node id.

targetRef.description: Target node ID, corresponding to BPMN node id.

conditionExpression.description: Flow condition expression (Flowable expression), must be wrapped in ${}. Available variables are limited to: 1) initiator: process initiator ID; 2) form.xxx: data table fields bound to the start node, e.g., form.totalDays, form.leaveType. The expression must return a boolean value, e.g.: ${form.totalDays > 3}. If not filled, it means unconditional flow (default sequence flow)

remark.description: Sequence flow remark, corresponding to <documentation> content in BPMN.
